channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Sofa left on Street, 13th May
item.description:
Sofa left on Street

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 41-48, Peacock Street, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Residents parking bay opposite 28 Fairhazel Gardens, 13th May
item.description:
Forest bike has been left on pavement and is now in residents parking bay opposite 28 Fairhazel Gardens. Bike has been there for about two weeks. Bike No. 30675
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 32, Fairhazel Gardens, South Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Disabled parking bay outside 36 Aberdare Gsrdens, 13th May
item.description:
Lime bike left on disabled parking bay. Bike No. ZNF-XDG
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 36, Aberdare Gardens, South Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wires exposed, 13th May
item.description:
Wires exposed on pavement for a months someone will trip over his in the dark
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Milk Street, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Alongside Grove View Apartments, on Chetwynd Road, 13th May
item.description:
Two Ash Trees heavily overgrown. They were incorrectly not pruned/pollarded in the last cycle (3yr+). Now request URGENT action to prevent damage to private property. Crown of both trees touch the Grove View Apartments building in windy conditions. Canopies intrude across the public/private land boundary. Canopies block the majority of natural light for multiple dwellings. Please arrange urgent tree maintenance.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chetwynd Road, Dartmouth Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Travel Lodge Eversholt Street one way towards Camden, 13th May
item.description:
At present, major HS2 roadworks are taking place in Eversholt Street, Euston, as a result, traffic is ONE WAY only from Euston towards Camden, in parts going down to a single lane. Unfortunately, the Rickshaws (Pedicabs) are ignoring the no entry signage from Camden towards Euston, and openly riding the wrong way down Eversholt Street, attached is the picture of the actual dashcam footage of the Rickshaw (Pedicab) that struck my vehicle breaking off the wing mirror, then dangerously sped off at speed, going illegally wrong way through Euston Bus Station, narrowly missing pedestrians, onto Euston Road (then I assume, back to the West End to pick up more passengers) I had just finished my shift at Euston Fire Station and have mentioned this to my colleagues, they mentioned that Fire Appliances have been delayed as a result of facing these rickshaws on route to fire calls towards Camden. As a fire fighter, when I finished my night shift, I watched Eversholt Street from the local cafe, in order to see the severity of this issue, and worryingly in the hour I was there, two rickshaws past me and travelling down Eversholt Street THEN back the WRONG WAY down the one way, luckily, no other vehicle was travelling on this road. As a priority, I firmly believe this section of Eversholt Street should be appropriately traffic managed and people caught illegally driving the wrong way through the roadworks one way street should be dealt with accordingly My other concern is, delays to fire appliances, further damage to vehicles, and most importantly, the pedestrian crossing located outside Euston train station is within the one way traffic works and a pedestrian may sustain injury by a rickshaw (or any other vehicle) no obeying the one way direction

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24, Eversholt Street, St Pancras, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside 66 Agamemnon Road, 13th May
item.description:
Report from There is a street tree outside 66 Agamemnon Road that has two small branches that are causing people to duck or walk around when they walk past it because they spurt out of the trunk at a low level. Would it be possible to get these two little branches cut off? Rest of the tree looks fine and the rest of its branches and canopy are causing no problems
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 60-94, Agamemnon Road, West Hampstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on wall, 13th May
item.description:
Graffiti on Mackintosh Lane, opposite 5 Mackintosh Lane

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Homerton High Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflown bin, 13th May
item.description:
This bin at the entrance to the woods is always overflowing with trash scattered everywhere. This pic is from this morning. The issue is ongoing. We need a permanent cleaner in our neighbourhood, the streets are filthy full of trash. There is a known issue with drug dealing and antisocial behaviour, at least help us keep our streets clean.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bastion Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Human waste outside the St Vincent's Brixton centre, 13th May
item.description:
Human waste was discovered on Monday 11 May. This was reported to Lambeth Council. The waste is still there today, Wednesday 13 May. I have previously been advised not to clear this myself, however I will need to as this is both a health hazard and offensive to our visitors and neighbours who live next to the centre.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Talma Road, Brixton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large polystyrene box outside 35 lakeswood road, 13th May
item.description:
A large polystyrene box has been dumped on the corner of lakewood road causing obstruction on pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lakeswood Road, Petts Wood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping in front of garages on Lucey Way, 13th May
item.description:
Witnessed a blue tipper truck deposit a load of black bags, a mattress and general rubbish in front of the garages on Lucey Way around 12:45 on 13th May 2026. The driver was white, with stubble and a blue baseball cap. This was also witnessed by someone riding a London Smoke & Cure delivery bike.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lucey Way, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter and polystyrene in road after refuse collection, 13th May
item.description:
Ridley Road junction Anthony Road. General waste bins collected this morning and broken polystyrene and litter is strewn all over the road and covering storm drains, looks like bin ejected load before it hit the hopper of the RCV
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ridley Road, East Wickham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress dumped, 13th May
item.description:
Becoming a frequent problem with fly tipping in Burrell Row.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Burrell Row, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large mattress left on pavement on Plum Lane, 13th May
item.description:
Yesterday, a large mattress was left on the pavement on Plum Lane and and is blocking the way for people walking on the pavement, including children and their parents on their way to and from school.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Plum Lane, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing communal bins and mixed recycling/waste, 13th May
item.description:
Communal bins are overflowing and household waste appears to be mixed with recycling. Waste has spilled onto the pavement. Close to 1 Swan Mead Estate .

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Swan Mead, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lime bikes blocking the pavement, 13th May
item.description:
A huge amount of Lime bikes have been left along one side of the concourse in front of Turnpike Lane station. There is a designated area for them. Which has been filled well beyond its boundaries. The bikes are also blocking the passageway that runs between the station and Westbury Avenue.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Westbury Avenue, Harringay, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping, 13th May
item.description:
Large bag of rubbish dumped in road leading into Beeches Close

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 106, Croydon Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipping large pallete on footway on Gipsy Hill, 13th May
item.description:
Flytipping large wooden pallete on footway outside Gipsy Hill rail station

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sainsbury Road, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead cat, 13th May
item.description:
